title: CDC NCHS Data Brief #535 — Births: Final Data for 2024
org: CDC / National Center for Health Statistics
notes: 3,628,934 U.S. births reported in 2024 final data (released July 2025).

title: Women Who Give Birth Incur Nearly $19000 in Additional Health Costs
notes: Women with employer-sponsored insurance who gave birth incurred ~$18,865 in additional pregnancy/birth/postpartum costs vs. women who did not give birth, of which ~$2,854 was out-of-pocket.

title: Health Costs Associated with Pregnancy Childbirth and Postpartum Care
notes: Brief updated September 9 2025 with 2021-2023 claims data. Vaginal delivery averages $15,712 total / $2,563 OOP; C-section averages $28,998 total / $3,071 OOP for employer-sponsored coverage.

title: Child Care Aware of America — 2024 Price of Care Landscape
notes: Most recent national price landscape; underpins state-by-state center / home / nanny ranges.

title: What Does It Cost to Raise a Child?
notes: USDA discontinued the Expenditures on Children by Families report after 2017. Used as historical context only.

title: AAP Bright Futures — Pediatric Periodicity Schedule
notes: Schedule for well-baby visits used to model first-year pediatric OOP costs.

title: AAP Policy Statement — Sleep-Related Infant Deaths: Updated 2022 Recommendations
notes: Source for room-share guidance and safe-sleep gear recommendations.

title: No Surprises Act — Overview
notes: Federal protections against surprise out-of-network billing.

title: Breastfeeding benefits — HealthCare.gov
org: U.S. Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services
notes: ACA-mandated coverage for a breast pump and lactation support.

title: Special enrollment for a newborn — HealthCare.gov
org: U.S. Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services
notes: Birth of a child triggers a 60-day special enrollment window.

title: Improving Child Care Access Affordability and Stability in CCDF
org: U.S. Department of Health & Human Services / ACF
notes: Source for the 7%-of-income childcare-affordability benchmark.

title: Retail Pricing Snapshot Q1 2026
confidence: Diaper, wipe, formula, gear ranges sampled across budget/mainstream/premium tiers (Target, Walmart, Costco, Amazon).

title: IRS Publication 503 (2026) — Child and Dependent Care Expenses
notes: Source for 2026 CDCTC rate schedule (50% max under OBBBA, 20% floor).

title: IRS Publication 15-B (2026) — Employer's Tax Guide to Fringe Benefits
notes: Source for 2026 dependent-care FSA cap: $7,500 ($3,750 MFS) under OBBBA.

title: Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act; Marketplace Integrity and Affordability — Final Rule
org: HHS / Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services
notes: Federal Register 2025-11606 (published June 25 2025). Establishes 2026 ACA maximum annual cost-sharing limits at $10,600 self-only / $21,200 other-than-self-only — revised upward from the earlier $10,150 / $20,300 figures in the original 2026 NBPP guidance.

title: Federal Family and Medical Leave Act
org: U.S. Department of Labor — Wage and Hour Division
notes: 12 weeks unpaid, job-protected leave at employers with 50+ employees.

title: State Paid Family & Medical Leave Program Pages
notes: Per-state benefit caps and weeks. Programs change annually; verify against official state portal before relying on a number.

title: California PFL + SDI (EDD)
notes: California Paid Family Leave under SB 951 — tiered 70%/90% wage replacement; 2026 max $1,765/wk.

title: Colorado FAMLI
notes: Colorado FAMLI 2026 — tiered 90%/50%; max $1,381.45/wk.

title: Connecticut Paid Leave
notes: CT Paid Leave — tiered 95%/60%; max $1,016.40/wk for 2026.

title: Delaware PFML
notes: DE PFML launched Jan 1 2026. Parental leave up to 12 wks @ 80%; medical/caregiver capped at 6 wks per 24 months. Max $900/wk.

title: DC Paid Family Leave
notes: DC PFL — 12 wks @ 90%; max $1,190/wk effective Sep 28 2025.

title: Hawaii TDI
notes: Hawaii TDI covers pregnancy disability only — no paid bonding leave yet.

title: Maryland FAMLI (delayed)
notes: Maryland FAMLI program delayed: contributions begin Jan 1 2027; benefits begin Jan 3 2028. Federal FMLA only for 2026.

title: Maine PFML
notes: Maine PFML benefits begin May 1 2026 — tiered 90%/66%; max $1,198.84/wk through 6/30/26.

title: Massachusetts PFML
org: Mass. Dept. of Family & Medical Leave
notes: MA PFML 2026 — tiered 80%/50%; max $1,230.39/wk.

title: Minnesota Paid Leave
notes: Minnesota Paid Leave launched Jan 1 2026 — max $1,423/wk.

title: New Hampshire Voluntary PFL
notes: Voluntary opt-in coverage; not a mandatory state program.

title: New Jersey Family Leave Insurance
notes: NJ FLI 2026 — 85% wage replacement; max $1,119/wk.

title: New York Paid Family Leave
notes: NY PFL 2026 — 67% of AWW; max $1,228.53/wk.

title: Paid Leave Oregon
notes: Paid Leave Oregon — tiered 100%/50% (100% up to 65% of SAWW); max $1,636.56/wk.

title: Rhode Island TCI
org: RI Department of Labor and Training
notes: RI TCI — 8 weeks (up from 7) effective Jan 1 2026; max $1,103/wk.

title: Vermont Voluntary PFL
notes: Voluntary state PFL via The Hartford; opt-in coverage.

title: Washington Paid Family & Medical Leave
notes: WA PFML 2026 — tiered 90%/50%; max $1,647/wk.

title: IRS Revenue Procedure 2025-32 (2026 inflation adjustments)
notes: Source for 2026 federal income tax bracket thresholds (single, MFJ, HOH, MFS). Published Oct 9 2025.
